title: Chapter 20: The Eve of Darkness
content: The dark moon rose over the Blackwood like a closed fist.

No moonlight. No starlight strong enough to penetrate the forest canopy. Just darkness — thick and absolute, the kind of darkness that felt alive. The kind that *was* alive, if Magnus's translations were correct.

The pack assembled at the ridge above the tomb entrance. Eighty warriors in wolf form, their fur bristling, their eyes gleaming in the darkness. Darius commanded the left flank. Kael commanded the center. And Sera...

Sera stood at the very edge of the ridge, looking down at the crack in the hillside that led to the seal chamber. Her silver wolf form was already in place, her mercury eyes fixed on the darkness below.
*Can you feel it?* Kael's thought pulsed through the bond.

*Yes.* She could feel it — a pressure in the air, a wrongness that made her fur stand on end. The seal was breaking. She could feel the silver symbols on the chamber walls flickering, dying, one by one. And behind them, something vast and ancient was pressing against the cracks.
*"The seal won't last another hour. Maybe less. We need to be ready."*
*We're ready.*

Darius's howl split the night — the signal. The first cracks had appeared in the hillside. Physical cracks, spiderwebbing across the rock face, glowing with that sickly green light that was the Shadow's signature.
And then the hillside *exploded*.

Not with fire or force — with *darkness*. A wave of absolute black poured out of the crack like water from a broken dam, spreading across the forest floor, swallowing trees and rocks and everything it touched. The trees didn't fall — they *dissolved*. The rocks didn't crack — they *vanished*. The darkness consumed everything.
"ARCHERS!" Darius roared. "LOOSE!"

A volley of silver-tipped arrows arced into the darkness. Most of them vanished without effect. A few — those that hit the densest part of the shadow — flared with silver light before being swallowed.
"It's not working!" one of the warriors shouted. "The arrows aren't doing anything!"
"Wait for it," Sera growled. "Wait for it to take form."
Because the darkness wasn't the Shadow. Not yet. This was just the *wake* — the first wave of its emergence, the shadow before the Shadow. When it took physical form...
The darkness in the valley began to *coalesce*.
It rose from the forest floor like smoke reversing its flow — tendrils of black pulling together, weaving into something solid. Something that had shape. Something that had *eyes*.
The First Shadow stood twenty feet tall.

It was vaguely humanoid — two legs, two arms, a head — but the proportions were wrong. Too long. Too thin. Its body was made of condensed darkness, shifting and swirling like oil on water. And its eyes — its eyes were the same sickly green as Aldric's magic, burning in the center of a featureless face.
*"MORTALS."*
The voice wasn't sound. It was a pressure in Sera's mind — a weight that pressed against her thoughts, cold and ancient and utterly without mercy.
*"YOU DARE TO STAND AGAINST ME? YOU DARE TO CHALLENGE WHAT HAS EXISTED SINCE BEFORE YOUR SPECIES CRAWLED OUT OF THE MUD?"*
"We dare," Sera said. Her voice was steady — steadier than she felt. "We're the Ironmoon Pack. And you're not welcome here."
The Shadow's burning eyes found her.
*"AH. THE MOON-MARKED. THE NEW GUARDIAN. I FELT YOU — WEEKS AGO, WHEN YOU TOUCHED MY SEAL. YOU'RE YOUNG. UNTRAINED. THE LAST ONE WAS STRONGER, AND SHE STILL COULDN'T DESTROY ME."*
"The last one didn't have what I have."
*"AND WHAT IS THAT?"*
Sera bared her teeth in a wolf's smile. "Backup."
She howled.
And the pack attacked.
The battle was chaos.

Eighty wolves against one ancient shadow entity — and it wasn't enough. The Shadow's limbs stretched like liquid darkness, swatting wolves aside like insects. Its touch was *wrong* — wolves who were grazed by the darkness collapsed, their fur turning grey, their strength draining away. It didn't kill them. It *consumed* them. Fed on their life force the way it had fed on forests and civilizations for millennia.
"Keep moving!" Darius shouted. "Don't let it touch you! Hit and run — wear it down!"

The warriors adapted. They'd trained for this — or something like it. They darted in, struck with silver claws and silver fangs, then darted away before the Shadow could retaliate. Each strike did minimal damage — a flicker of silver light, a momentary disruption in the darkness — but dozens of strikes, hundreds of strikes...
The Shadow was slowing.
*"CLEVER. BUT NOT CLEVER ENOUGH."*

Its form shifted. The humanoid shape dissolved into a wave of darkness that swept across the battlefield — not targeting the wolves this time, but *surrounding* them. Cutting them off from each other. Isolating them in pockets of shadow.
"SERA!" Kael's voice cut through the chaos. "The fire! NOW!"
Sera reached deep inside herself — past her wolf, past her fear, past everything — and found the Silver Fire.
It erupted from her body like a star being born.

Cold. Silver. Blazing. The darkness around her *screamed* — an actual, audible scream, the first sound the Shadow had made that wasn't telepathic pressure. The fire spread outward, burning through the tendrils of darkness that had isolated the pack, reuniting the warriors, driving the Shadow back toward the center of the valley.
*"WHAT — THIS POWER — THE LAST ONE DIDN'T HAVE THIS POWER — "*
"I'm not the last one!" Sera shouted, advancing on the Shadow with fire sheeted around her like armor. "I'm the NEW one! And I'm not alone!"

Kael was beside her — the black Alpha wolf, his golden eyes blazing, his fur singed where the Silver Fire had touched him but his determination unbroken. And behind them, the pack rallied — warriors pulling themselves up from where the Shadow had thrown them, forming ranks, advancing as one.
The Shadow retreated.
For the first time in a thousand years, the ancient darkness *retreated*.
*"THIS IS NOT OVER. YOU CANNOT DESTROY ME. I HAVE EXISTED SINCE BEFORE TIME. I WILL EXIST AFTER YOUR PACK IS DUST AND YOUR MOON HAS CRUMBLED TO ASH."*
"We'll see about that," Sera growled — and lunged.
The Silver Fire and the black wolf struck the Shadow together.
And the darkness *howled*.
